codeReview.summary: The feature delivers the requested scaffold (pyproject, package structure, models, config, storage, env template, gitignore, and tests) and covers the expected behaviors. I found two non-blocking setup inconsistencies.

issues.description: The console script entry point is set to snipsnap.cli:main, but this commit does not include snipsnap/cli.py. The installed snipsnap command would fail until the later CLI feature is implemented.
Project metadata references README.md, but no README.md is present in this commit. Packaging metadata points to a missing file.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The backend-worker skill requires tests-first (RED), but this bootstrap feature created implementation files before tests while still reporting followedProcedure=true. The skill/process should explicitly account for initial scaffold features where strict tests-first is impractical.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/backend-worker/SKILL.md (Work Procedure step 2) vs transcript skeleton for workerSessionId a93e4f38-9e9e-4369-8e82-1420015a7450 showing Create calls for pyproject/snipsnap files before tests/test_models.py and tests/test_storage.py; handoff.skillFeedback.suggestedChanges also notes procedure mismatch for setup features.

summary: Pass. The core project setup requirements are implemented and validated, with only minor non-blocking packaging/setup inconsistencies.
